articles.title: ADS IN APPLE MAPS ARE NOW OFFICIAL, AND THEY'RE COMING THIS SUMMER
articles.summary: Apple has officially announced that ads are coming to Apple Maps in the US and Canada starting this summer, ending one of its key advantages over competitors like Google Maps. The ads will appear as paid listings in search results and suggested places, clearly labeled and based on relevance rather than just payment amount. Apple claims the ads will maintain user privacy by keeping data on-device and not tying it to Apple IDs, though this marks a significant shift toward monetizing their services.

articles.title: DESIGN WITH AGENTS ON THE FIGMA CANVAS, NOW IN BETA
articles.summary: Figma has launched a beta feature that allows AI agents to create and modify design assets directly on the Figma canvas using existing components, variables, and tokens. The AI agents first read design libraries to ensure their outputs reflect the established design system. This feature is available across platforms, including Claude, VS Code, Cursor, and others, with customizable skills that can be authored by anyone familiar with Figma.

articles.title: APPLE RELEASES IOS 26.4 WITH LONG-AWAITED KEYBOARD FIX
articles.summary: iOS 26.4 is a refinement-focused update that improves everyday iPhone use, led by a long-awaited keyboard accuracy fix that reduces mistypes and autocorrect issues. It also enhances Apple Music with AI-generated playlists and concert discovery, adds offline song recognition via Shazam, introduces video playback in Apple Podcasts, expands accessibility options, enables individual payments in Family Sharing, and allows voice AI apps in CarPlay. Alongside small additions like new emoji, the update also patches 34 security vulnerabilities, making it a worthwhile install despite lacking major changes like a revamped Siri.

articles.title: THE METAVERSE DIED, BUT SPATIAL DESIGN DIDN'T (WHAT DESIGNERS CAN TAKE AWAY)
articles.summary: Meta shut down Horizon Worlds VR after investing $80 billion, marking the death of the traditional "metaverse" vision that nobody actually wanted. However, spatial design principles remain valuable as the industry shifts toward AR glasses, Apple's Vision Pro, and spatial computing applications that enhance reality rather than replace it. Designers should focus on spatial thinking for lightweight AR experiences and tools that enhance real-world activities rather than creating escapist virtual worlds.

articles.title: WHAT HAPPENS TO THE DESIGN SYSTEM WHEN AI CHANGES THE PRODUCT?
articles.summary: AI is reshaping design systems by shifting users from step-by-step “operators” to decision-makers who review and act on AI-generated suggestions, making traditional components like forms and linear workflows less central. While foundational elements (like typography and accessibility) remain stable, design systems must evolve to support new interaction patterns such as suggestions, mixed AI/manual control, uncertainty handling, and trust repair. In the long term, they may move away from fixed component libraries toward flexible rules or constraints, with consistency defined more by user understanding than identical interfaces.

articles.title: AR GLASSES ARE HERE, BUT WHAT ABOUT ACCESSIBILITY?
articles.summary: New AR glasses from companies like Meta and XREAL are becoming more practical, but their growth highlights the need to design accessibility from the start rather than as an add-on. While current features like speech-to-text and tools such as Be My Eyes already help, AR's real potential lies in universal, multisensory design—using vision, sound, and touch together to improve understanding and usability. This approach can make AR experiences more intuitive, inclusive, and immersive for everyone, not just users with disabilities.

articles.title: BUILD WEBSITES AND APPS WITH AI
articles.summary: Anima is the design-aware AI agent that bridges creativity and code. It generates real, production-ready apps from Figma files, URLs, or prompts for product teams building directly or coding platforms integrating via API.

articles.title: VISUAL JSON EDITOR
articles.summary: Visual-json is a visual JSON editor that provides an intuitive interface for editing JSON files. The tool offers multiple view modes, including Tree, Raw, and package.json views for better visualization and editing.

articles.title: EASY DATA VISUALIZATIONS
articles.summary: Embedful is a platform that allows users to quickly turn data into client-ready charts, tables, and dashboards without needing engineers or expensive BI tools. Users can connect various data sources, such as Google Analytics, CSV files, or Excel sheets, and create visualizations that can be shared via links or embedded anywhere.

articles.title: DESIGNING BEYOND THE CHECKLIST
articles.summary: The Firefox UX team emphasizes that accessibility should go beyond checklists and compliance requirements to become a fundamental design mindset. Through user research with the Fable platform and direct connections with people using assistive technologies, the team discovered crucial insights into magnification software, voice control, and other accessibility needs that traditional audits miss. This approach has transformed their design process, ensuring accessibility is considered from the beginning rather than as a final review step, aligning with Mozilla's mission to make the internet open and usable for everyone.

articles.title: ROCKET LEAGUE'S ‘NEW' LOGO IS ONE BIG GAME OF SPOT THE DIFFERENCE
articles.summary: The esports team Ninjas in Pyjamas drew mixed reactions after unveiling a “new” logo for Rocket League that appears almost indistinguishable from the original design. While logo redesigns usually introduce noticeable changes, this update was so subtle that fans debated whether anything had changed at all, turning the announcement into an ironic and somewhat humorous moment. The situation highlights both the expectations around branding updates and how even minor—or seemingly nonexistent—tweaks can spark strong community discussion.

articles.title: UNTOUCHED SINCE THE 1980S, JORDAN'S FURNITURE GETS A SLEEK REBRAND WITH ERGONOMIC ICONS
articles.summary: Jordan's Furniture has undergone its first major visual refresh in over 30 years, led by Allie Hughes and TBWA\Chiat\Day, aiming to modernize the brand while preserving its personality and strong community identity. The redesign centers on a more human, playful “J” symbol with a signature “wink,” a simplified wordmark that drops “furniture,” and a system of icons derived from the logo, while retaining a vibrant color palette inspired by its classic look. Updated typography and visual elements strike a balance between fresh and familiar, resulting in a brand that feels more contemporary, approachable, and flexible beyond traditional furniture.

articles.title: THIS ARTIST PUTS A PAINTERLY SPIN ON GEORGE R. R. MARTIN'S GAME OF THRONES PREQUEL
articles.summary: Chinese concept artist Jin Dongyu creates painterly fan art inspired by George R.R. Martin's "A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms" prequel.

articles.title: EIGHT EYE-CATCHING TYPEFACES THAT WILL ELEVATE YOUR EDITORIAL DESIGNS
articles.summary: Creative Boom highlights eight distinctive typefaces, including Big Sans, which shifts from clean to expressive with stylistic sets, and Druto, engineered for minimal line spacing without sacrificing readability.
